title: Equity
author: Minal Bopaiah
subline: How to design organizations where everyone thrives.
customHTML:
<svg viewBox="0 0 1600 2400" preserveAspectRatio="xMidYMid slice" style="position:absolute;inset:0;width:100%;height:100%">
  <!-- The classic 'three figures behind a fence' equity illustration: three figures of different heights, with the fence panels at different heights so they can all see over. The third figure is RED — that's the equity adjustment. -->
  <g transform="translate(800 1700)" fill="none">
    <!-- Ground line -->
    <line x1="-600" y1="200" x2="600" y2="200" stroke="#4c4c4c" stroke-width="2"/>
    <!-- Figure 1 (tallest) — ash -->
    <g stroke="#4c4c4c" stroke-width="2" stroke-linecap="round">
      <circle cx="-380" cy="80" r="36"/>
      <line x1="-380" y1="116" x2="-380" y2="200"/>
    </g>
    <!-- Fence board 1 (low — tall figure looks over) -->
    <line x1="-460" y1="200" x2="-460" y2="100" stroke="#4c4c4c" stroke-width="3"/>
    <line x1="-300" y1="200" x2="-300" y2="100" stroke="#4c4c4c" stroke-width="3"/>
    <line x1="-460" y1="100" x2="-300" y2="100" stroke="#4c4c4c" stroke-width="3"/>
    <!-- Figure 2 (medium) — ash -->
    <g stroke="#4c4c4c" stroke-width="2" stroke-linecap="round">
      <circle cx="0" cy="120" r="30"/>
      <line x1="0" y1="150" x2="0" y2="200"/>
    </g>
    <!-- Fence board 2 (medium) -->
    <line x1="-80" y1="200" x2="-80" y2="140" stroke="#4c4c4c" stroke-width="3"/>
    <line x1="80" y1="200" x2="80" y2="140" stroke="#4c4c4c" stroke-width="3"/>
    <line x1="-80" y1="140" x2="80" y2="140" stroke="#4c4c4c" stroke-width="3"/>
    <!-- Figure 3 (shortest) — RED, with a RED step/box that gives them the height to see over -->
    <g stroke="#fc1c46" stroke-width="2" stroke-linecap="round">
      <circle cx="380" cy="160" r="26"/>
      <line x1="380" y1="186" x2="380" y2="200"/>
    </g>
    <!-- The RED step (equity intervention) -->
    <rect x="320" y="170" width="120" height="30" fill="#fc1c46" stroke="#fc1c46" stroke-width="2"/>
    <!-- Fence board 3 (tall) — ash -->
    <line x1="300" y1="200" x2="300" y2="60" stroke="#4c4c4c" stroke-width="3"/>
    <line x1="460" y1="200" x2="460" y2="60" stroke="#4c4c4c" stroke-width="3"/>
    <line x1="300" y1="60" x2="460" y2="60" stroke="#4c4c4c" stroke-width="3"/>
    <!-- A horizontal 'sight line' showing all three figures can see over the top -->
    <line x1="-380" y1="40" x2="380" y2="40" stroke="#cccccc" stroke-width="0.8" stroke-dasharray="4 4" opacity="0.5"/>
  </g>
  <text x="800" y="2200" text-anchor="middle" font-family="'Sui','Söhne','Inter',system-ui,sans-serif" font-size="14" font-weight="500" fill="#4c4c4c" letter-spacing="0.4em">EQUAL INPUT · UNEQUAL SUPPORT · ONE SIGHT LINE</text>
</svg>
rationale: The classic equity illustration: three figures of different heights behind three fence panels. The third (shortest) figure is RED and stands on a RED step — the equity intervention. A dashed sight line runs across the top showing all three figures can see over. Title 480px mixed case, single word 'Equity', weight 500.
